quiz.name: Session 09 Entry Quiz: SOC Workflow and Automation
quiz.intro: Assess prerequisite knowledge before the session. 6 multiple-choice questions. Select the single best answer.

questiontext.text: An analyst receives 200 security alerts during an 8-hour shift and manages to review 80 of them. The remaining 120 are ignored. What is this phenomenon called?

feedback.text: Correct. Alert fatigue occurs when analysts are overwhelmed by volume and stop reviewing all alerts, causing real threats to be missed.

questiontext.text: A security event was generated by an IDS rule. After investigation, the analyst confirms it is a real attack. What is the correct classification?

feedback.text: Correct. A true positive is a real security event that is correctly flagged by a detection rule.

questiontext.text: Which of the following best describes a SOAR platform?

answer.text: A tool for storing and searching log files
A vulnerability scanner that identifies weaknesses in systems
A platform that automates security workflows and integrates security tools

feedback.text: Correct. SOAR stands for Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response. It connects security tools and automates repetitive tasks.

answer.text: An intrusion detection system based on network signatures

questiontext.text: What does MTTR stand for in SOC operations?

feedback.text: Correct. MTTR measures the average time from when an incident is detected to when it is resolved.

questiontext.text: A Tier 1 analyst has been investigating an alert for 25 minutes and cannot determine if it is a true positive or false positive. According to standard SOC procedure, what should they do?

answer.text: Close the alert as a false positive to meet the SLA
Continue investigating until they are certain
Delete the alert from the queue and re-check tomorrow

feedback.text: Correct. When an L1 analyst cannot resolve uncertainty within the SLA window, the correct action is to escalate — not force-close or continue indefinitely.

questiontext.text: In a SOC RACI matrix, a Tier 1 analyst is listed as R (Responsible) for alert triage and I (Informed) for threat hunting. What does this mean in practice?

answer.text: Tier 1 must approve all threat hunting activities before they begin
Tier 1 performs alert triage independently and is notified of threat hunting outcomes but does not participate

feedback.text: Correct. In a RACI matrix, R (Responsible) means the role performs the task; I (Informed) means they receive updates only and are not involved in execution.

answer.text: Tier 1 is responsible for threat hunting if no Tier 3 analyst is available
Tier 1 must consult Tier 3 before closing any alert
